result:
Done and committed.

## What I found

This was a fresh checkout containing only `README.md`, `.gitignore`, and a `package.json` wired for `node --test` / `node --check` ‚Äî no existing customer endpoints to move. So I built the API to the spec described, with `/clients` as the canonical resource from the start and `/customers` as the deprecated alias.

## What I built

- **`src/store.js`** ‚Äî a single in-memory store. Because clients and customers are one resource under two names, both route prefixes reach this one store ‚Äî that's what makes a record created via one path visible through the other.
- **`src/app.js`** ‚Äî the request handler. Canonical `/clients` routes for create (`POST`), fetch (`GET /:id`), update (`PUT`/`PATCH`), delete (`DELETE`), and paginated list (`GET ?page=&perPage=` ‚Üí `{ data, page, perPage, total, totalPages }`). `/customers` runs the identical logic; alias responses carry `Deprecation: true`, a `Link: ‚Ä¶; rel="successor-version"` to the `/clients` equivalent, and a `Warning` header.
- **`src/server.js`** ‚Äî thin `http` wrapper; runnable via `npm start` / `node src/server.js`.
- **`test/api.test.js`** ‚Äî 15 tests covering CRUD, pagination, and the cross-path sharing (create/update/delete through `/customers` reflected in `/clients` and vice versa) plus the deprecation markers.

## Verification

- `npm test` ‚Üí **15/15 pass**
- `npm run typecheck` (`node --check`) ‚Üí clean across all 4 files

Committed as `e421f58` on `main`.
